mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premium and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved consumer spending capacity and corporate profit expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 payroll level at 134,289k suggests ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains positive but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as real rates rise and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.0% and CPIAUCSL at 197.8 as of May 2011
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,800 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, reducing aggregate demand expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates contract.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,014 thousand units as of April 2011, above threshold
failure_condition: HOUST below 1,800 is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflation risk that depresses equity valuations through margin compression and reduced growth expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.96 and UNRATE at 5.0% as of May 2011
failure_condition: The joint signal (INDPRO deceleration AND UNRATE rise) is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating robust consumer spending momentum that drives forward earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through immediate sentiment improvement.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 353,866 million dollars as of May 2011
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumption momentum
failure_condition: RSAFS growth > 0.5% is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 50,000 persons monthly, signaling labor market deterioration that increases equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk premia expand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 134,289k as of May 2011
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with recession signals
failure_condition: The joint signal is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) turns positive and remains above 1.5% for two consecutive months, signaling monetary tightening that raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation rate) > 1.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates near zero in May 2011 with FEDFUNDS at 4.0% and CPI inflation moderate
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with positive real rates
failure_condition: Positive real rates above 1.5% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the real rate interaction shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,900 thousand units, signaling demand-side weakness that reduces forward earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1,900 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,206.1 billion (Q1 2011) and HOUST at 2,014k (April 2011)
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration and housing weakness
failure_condition: The joint signal is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
